channel.title: The New Beverly Cinema
channel.description: Schedule and program for The New Beverly Cinema (Quentin Tarantino's revival theater in Los Angeles).

item.title: Cabaret (IB Tech Print)
item.description:
Winner of 8 Academy Awards, including Best Actress and Best Director, director / choreographer Bob Fosse’s adaptation of the Broadway show is a stylish, big screen musical masterpiece. Set in 1931 Berlin, Cabaret chronicles young American Sally Bowles (Liza Minnelli) and her bohemian life as a performer at the Kit Kat Klub during the last days of the Weimar Republic, as well as her relationships with a reserved academic (Michael York) and a rich playboy (Helmut Griem). Minnelli is electric in her star-making role and a stellar Joel Grey shines in his Best Supporting Actor performance as the bawdy Master of Ceremonies. “A great movie musical. Taking its form from political cabaret, it’s a satire of temptations. In a prodigious balancing act, Bob Fosse, the choreographer-director, keeps the period – Berlin, 1931 – at a cool distance. We see the decadence as garish and sleazy; yet we also see the animal energy in it – everything seems to become sexualized. The movie does not exploit decadence; rather, it gives it its due. With Joel Grey as our devil-doll host – the master of ceremonies – and Liza Minnelli (in her first singing role on the screen) as exuberant, corruptible Sally Bowles, chasing after the life of a headliner no matter what; Minnelli has such gaiety and electricity that she becomes a star before our eyes.” – Pauline Kael, The New Yorker “ Cabaret is most unusual: it is literate, bawdy, sophisticated, sensual, cynical, heart-warming, and disturbingly thought-provoking” – Variety “This is no ordinary musical. Part of its success comes because it doesn’t fall for the old cliché that musicals have to make you happy. Instead of cheapening the movie version by lightening its load of despair, director Bob Fosse has gone right to the bleak heart of the material and stayed there well enough to win an Academy Award for Best Director.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Bob Fosse | Writer: Screenplay by Jay Allen, based on the musical play "Cabaret" book by Joe Masteroff based on the play by John Van Druten and stories by Christopher Isherwood | Starring: Liza Minnelli, Michael York, Helmut Griem, Marisa Berenson, Joel Grey | Year: 1972 | Country: USA | Format: I.B. Technicolor 35mm
Durata: 123 minuti

Orari: Fri August 14 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Cannibal Holocaust
item.description:
An anthropologist ventures to South America in search of a group who went missing while filming a documentary about cannibal tribes. And when he finds their lost footage, it will contain the shocking truth about their gruesome fate. The most controversial movie ever made has lost none of its unflinching power, a cruel cinematic landmark featuring special effects so realistic that director Ruggero Deodato was arrested on suspicion of murder.
Cast: Director: Ruggero Deodato | Writer: Story and Screenplay by Gianfranco Clerici | Starring: Robert Kerman, Francesca Ciardi, Perry Pirkanen, Luca Barbareschi | Year: 1980 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Sat August 08 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Ball of Fire / The Lady Eve
item.description:
Gary Cooper & Barbara Stanwyck star in a comedy classic from director Howard Hawks and screenwriters Charles Brackett and Billy Wilder! On the run, a burlesque dancer hides out in a house where eight stuffy professors are compiling an encyclopaedia on current American slang… and gets taken on as an expert in “slanguage.” (Park Circus) The perfect date night delight! Preston Sturges expertly mixes superb screwball situations and gut-busting giggles with sweet-natured romance and some of the best on-screen chemistry ever captured on film. After a year-long expedition up the Amazon, a naive heir to a brewery fortune (Henry Fonda) finds himself aboard a luxury liner and in the sites of a trio of sharp-witted card sharps. But fate has other plans when the beautiful con artist (Barbara Stanwyck) begins to fall for her mark.
Cast: Director: Howard Hawks | Writer: Screen play by Charles Brackett and Billy Wilder from an original story by Billy Wilder and Thomas Monroe | Starring: Gary Cooper, Barbara Stanwyck | Year: 1941 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Preston Sturges | Writer: Preston Sturges based on a story by Monckton Hoffe | Starring: Barbara Stanwyck, Henry Fonda, Charles Coburn, Eugene Pallette, Martha O'Driscoll, William Demarest, Eric Blore | Year: 1941 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 94 minuti

Orari: Tue August 18 - 7:30 pm / 9:50 pm

item.title: The 39 Steps (1935) / Sabotage (1936)
item.description:
A heart-racing spy story by Alfred Hitchcock, The 39 Steps follows Richard Hannay (Robert Donat) as he stumbles upon a conspiracy that thrusts him into a hectic chase across the Scottish moors – a chase in which he is both the pursuer and the pursued – as well as into an unexpected romance with the cool Pamela (Madeline Carroll). This classic wrong-man thriller from the Master of Suspense anticipates the director’s most famous works (especially North by Northwest ), and remains one of his cleverest and most entertaining films. (American Genre Film Archive) One of the most playful of Hitchcock’s British thrillers, Sabotage doesn’t waste one second of its taut 77-minute runtime, ratcheting up the suspense in pointillistically beautiful ways. Sylvia Sidney is the unsuspecting wife of a London cinema owner (Oskar Homolka) whom a Scotland Yard detective (John Loder) comes to believe is behind a string of terrorist attacks. The gripping centerpiece – involving a boy, a film canister and a ticking time bomb – is one of the most heart-stopping moments of pure suspense in all of Hitchcock’s canon. (American Genre Film Archive)
Cast: Director: Alfred Hitchcock | Starring: Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle, Peggy Ashcroft | Year: 1935 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm | Director: Alfred Hitchcock | Starring: Sylvia Sidney, Oskar Homolka, Desmond Tester, John Loder | Year: 1936 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 76 minuti

Orari: Fri August 28 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

Orari: Sat August 15 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Moneyball
item.description:
Oakland A’s general manager Billy Beane (Brad Pitt) challenges the system and defies conventional wisdom when he is forced to rebuild his small-market team, on a limited budget. Despite opposition from the old guard, the media, fans and their own manager (Philip Seymour Hoffman), Beane – with the help of a young, number-crunching, Yale-educated economist (Jonah Hill) – develops a roster of misfits… and along the way, forever changes the way the game is played. (Sony Pictures)
Cast: Director: Bennett Miller | Writer: Screenplay bySteven Zaillian and Aaron Sorkin, story by Stan Chervin, based on the book by Michael Lewis | Starring: Brad Pitt, Jonah Hill, Philip Seymour Hoffman, Robin Wright, Chris Pratt | Year: 2011 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 133 minuti

Orari: Fri August 21 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Knights of the Round Table
item.description:
All the glory and splendor of King Arthur’s court presented in massive CinemaScope! The Arthurian legend receives a lavish Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer adaptation, lusciously lensed by Freddie Young ( Lawrence of Arabia ) & Stephen Dade ( Zulu ), and featuring a stirring Miklos Rozsa score. Sir Lancelot (Robert Taylor) is sworn to serve King Arthur (Mel Ferrer), but his devotion to the queen (Ava Gardner) could provide fuel for their enemies to destroy Camelot.
Cast: Director: Richard Thorpe | Writer: Screen play by Talbot Jennings & Jan Lustig and Noel Langley | Starring: Robert Taylor, Ava Gardner, Mel Ferrer, Anne Crawford, Stanley Baker, Felix Aylmer, Maureen Swanson | Year: 1953 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 115 minuti

Orari: Sun August 16 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Pulp Fiction
item.description:
Two hitmen out to retrieve a suitcase stolen. An aging boxer paid to lose his next fight. A couple of young lovers and small-time thieves who decide they need a change of venue. Their lives are woven together in the exhilarating Pulp Fiction . Quentin Tarantino’s Palme d’Or-winning classic is packed with electric dialogue, potent pop-culture touchstones, and an unforgettable ensemble cast. You won’t know the facts until you’ve seen the fiction. “A spectacularly entertaining piece of pop culture, Pulp Fiction is the American Graffiti of violent crime pictures.” – Todd McCarthy, Variety “ Pulp Fiction crackles like an ozone generator: Here’s a director who’s been let loose inside the toy store, and wants to play all night. 4 Stars ” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Written by Quentin Tarantino, Stories by Quentin Tarantino & Roger Avary | Starring: John Travolta, Samuel L. Jackson, Uma Thurman, Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Amanda Plummer, Maria de Medeiros, Ving Rhames, Eric Stoltz, Rosanna Arquette, Christopher Walken and Bruce Willis | Year: 1994 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 154 minuti

Orari: Fri August 28 - 11:59 pm

item.title: School of Rock
item.description:
In the role he was born to play, Jack Black is Dewey Finn, a slacker guitar player still holding on to the dream of being a rock star. Due to a mix-up (and a low-level con), the wildly unqualified Dewey ends up substitute teacher in a prestigious elementary school. He recruits the bored prep school kids to form a new band, so these talented kids can rock the battle of the bands like it’s never been rocked before. This hilariously quoteable contemporary classic made Jack Black a superstar. And of course, it’s got a killer rock n rock soundtrack featuring songs from Led Zeppelin, Cream, T. Rex and the Black Keys, not to mention the original anthems performed by the kids themselves. Let’s get rockin! “If quirky, independent, grown-up outsider filmmakers set out to make a family movie, this is the kind of movie they would make. And they did.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Richard Linklater | Writer: Mike White | Starring: Jack Black, Joan Cusack, Mike White, Sarah Silverman, Miranda Cosgrove | Year: 2003 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 109 minuti

Orari: Sat August 08 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Throw Momma from the Train / Ruthless People
item.description:
Danny DeVito and Billy Crystal star in a black comedy riff on Hitchcock’s Strangers on a Train ! Crystal plays a newly divorced sad sack bitter that his ex-wife stole his novel and turned it into her own bestseller. DeVito is the long-suffering momma’s boy eager to get out from his mother’s thumb. But when one suggests trading murders as the perfect alibi, the foul play will spin hilariously out of control. DeVito directs his first theatrical feature film, while director of photography Barry Sonnenfeld adds a stylish & energetic verve to the proceedings (the same year he also helmed Raising Arizona ). Plus, Anne Ramsey is unforgettable as DeVito’s domineering mother. In this fiendishly funny comedy from the creators of Airplane! , loathsome millionaire Sam Stone (Danny DeVito) is RUTHLESS. How ruthless? When his wife Barbara (Bette Midler) is nabbed by inept kidnappers (Judge Reinhold, Helen Slater), Sam cries tears of joy… and refuses to pay the ransom. And when the abductors threaten to kill the abrasive heiress, Sam takes immediate action – he celebrates! Ruthless People . Raucous… outlandish… one of the top box office hits of the year! (Touchstone Pictures)
Cast: Director: Danny DeVito | Writer: Stu Silver | Starring: Danny DeVito, Billy Crystal, Kim Greist, Anne Ramsey | Year: 1987 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Jim Abrahams, David Zucker, Jerry Zucker | Writer: Dale Launer | Starring: Danny DeVito, Judge Reinhold, Helen Slater, Bette Midler | Year: 1986 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 94 minuti

Orari: Sun August 02 - 6:30 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: Ice Cream Man
item.description:
An idyllic summer town descends into madness when an ice cream man serves kids sweet delights with horrifying results. (The Horror Section)
Cast: Director: Eli Roth | Writer: Screenplay by Eli Roth and Noah Belson, story by Eli Roth | Starring: Ari Millen, Charlie Zeltzer, Shiloh O'Reilly, Kiori Mirza Waldman, Sarah Abbott, Benjamin Byron Davis | Year: 2026 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 86 minuti

Orari: Tue August 11 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Midnight Run / 48 Hrs.
item.description:
The odd couple classic! Robert De Niro is a tough ex-cop tasked with finding Charles Grodin, a mild-mannered mob accountant who’s skipped bail and on the lam. But what was supposed to be a simple Midnight Run turns into a comedic, action-packed cross country chase where he’ll have to get the FBI off his case, the mob off his trail, and Grodin off his back! “ Midnight Run shows that Robert De Niro can be as wonderful in a comic role as he is in a serious one. Pair him, a gruff ex-cop and bounty hunter, with straight man Charles Grodin, his captive, and the result is one of the most entertaining, best executed, original road pictures ever.” – Variety “Whoever cast De Niro and Grodin must have had a sixth sense for the chemistry they would have; they work together so smoothly, and with such an evident sense of fun, that even their silences are intriguing.” – Roger Ebert The boys are back in town. Nick Nolte is a cop. Eddie Murphy is a convict. They couldn’t have liked each other less… They couldn’t have needed each other more. And the last place they every expected to be is on the same side. Even for 48 Hrs. Walter Hill’s exhilarating action comedy is one of the best buddy team-ups of the decade, with Murphy positively electric in his film debut.
Cast: Director: Martin Brest | Writer: George Gallo | Starring: Robert De Niro, Charles Grodin, Yaphet Kotto, John Ashton, Dennis Farina, Joe Pantoliano | Year: 1988 | Director: Walter Hill | Writer: Roger Spottiswoode and Walter Hill & Larry Gross and Steven E. de Souza | Starring: Nick Nolte, Eddie Murphy, Annette O'Toole, Frank McRae, James Remar, David Patrick Kelly, Sonny Landham, Brion James | Year: 1982 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Wed August 05 - 7:30 pm / 10:05 pm

item.title: Dredd
item.description:
In a hyper-violent dystopic metropolis, an elite law enforcement group acts as judge, jury, and executioner. And when veteran officer Judge Dredd (Karl Urban) and his young recruit (Olivia Thirlby) arrive at a 200-storey tower block to investigate a murder, they’ll find themselves in a ruthless battle with a vicious drug kingpin (Lena Headey) and her seemingly endless army of killers. Screenwriter Alex Garland adapts the legendary comic.
Cast: Director: Pete Travis | Writer: Screenplay by Alex Garland based on 'Judge Dredd' created by John Wagner and Carlos Ezquerra | Starring: Karl Urban, Olivia Thirlby, Wood Harris, Lena Headey | Year: 2012 | Country: UK/South Africa | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Sat August 01 - 11:59 pm

item.title: To Live and Die in L.A. / The Rip
item.description:
A federal agent is dead. A killer is loose. And the City of Angels is about to explode. In one of the slickest thrillers ever to hit the streets of Los Angeles, a Secret Service agent ( C.S.I. ‘s William Petersen) vows to do whatever it takes in search of the counterfeiter (Willem Dafoe) who killed his partner. To Live and Die in L.A. is razor sharp and exquisitely stylized, William Friedkin’s West Coast mic drop on mid-80s Miami Vice mania, featuring a pulsing score by Wang Chung and sizzling cinematography by Robby Muller. Every scene feels authentic and dangerously alive; the film was scripted by an actual Secret Service agent and ex-cons were consulted to make sure the counterfeiting was done right. Plus, it’s got one of the all-time great car chases and an incendiary finale that will leave you breathless! “William Friedkin was making sleek, sensational, ice-cold thrillers long before rock videos and Miami Vice were fashionable. And he couldn’t be more in his element now that they are. To Live and Die in L.A. is Mr. Friedkin at his glossiest, a great-looking, riveting movie” – Janet Maslin, The New York Times “In the hierarchy of great movie chase sequences, the recent landmarks include the chases under the Brooklyn elevated tracks in The French Connection and down the hills of San Francisco in Bullitt … [ To Live and Die In L.A. ] contains another chase that belongs on that short list. 4 Stars! ” – Roger Ebert “An exciting cop thriller with a novel premise that manages to deliver the goods in really clever ways. The whole package worked for me” – Quentin Tarantino Trust frays when a team of Miami cops discovers millions in cash inside a run-down stash house, calling everyone – and everything – into question. (Netflix)
Cast: Director: William Friedkin | Writer: screenplay by William Friedkin & Gerald Petievich based on the novel by Gerald Petievich | Starring: William Petersen, Willem Dafoe, John Pankow, Debra Feuer, John Turturro, Darlanne Fluegel, Dean Stockwell, Steve James, Robert Downey Sr | Year: 1985 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe Carnahan | Writer: Screenplay by Joe Carnahan, story by Joe Carnahan & Michael McGrale | Starring: Matt Damon, Ben Affleck, Steven Yeun, Teyana Taylor, Catalina Sandino Moreno, Sasha Calle, Kyle Chandler, Scott Adkins | Year: 2026 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 113 minuti

Orari: Tue August 25 - 7:30 pm / 9:55 pm

Jackson, Uma Thurman, Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Amanda Plummer, Maria de Medeiros, Ving Rhames, Eric Stoltz, Rosanna Arquette, Christopher Walken and Bruce Willis | Year: 1994 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 154 minuti

Orari: Fri August 21 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The Thrill Killers / Psychedelic Sexualis
item.description:
Homicidal maniacs on a bloody rampage! In director Ray Dennis Steckler’s cult mindblower, a trio of psychotic killers escape from a mental intuition and wreak havoc across Los Angeles. Liz Renay ( Desperate Living ), Steckler regular Carolyn Brandt, legendary stuntman Gary Kent, and Steckler himself (under his Cash Flagg alias) star. A deranged sniper shoots at cars on a freeway, only to commit suicide as the police close in. In a series of flashbacks, the people who knew this disturbed young man reflect on what might have caused him to embark on his deadly rampage. Directed by Albert Zugsmith (Confessions of an Opium Eater), On Her Bed of Roses is an odd blending of post-noir, Vietnam era character drama and drive-in sleaze. (Vinegar Syndrome) aka On Her Bed of Roses
Cast: Director: Ray Dennis Steckler | Starring: Cash Flagg [Ray Dennis Steckler], Liz Renay, Brick Bardo, Carolyn Brandt, Gary Kent | Year: 1964 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Albert Zugsmith | Starring: Ronald Warren, Sandra Lynn, Barbara Hines, Lee Gladden | Year: 1966 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 101 minuti

Orari: Mon August 17 - 7:30 pm / 9:10 pm

item.title: Misery / Dead Calm (Special Guests)
item.description:
While our allotment of advance tickets has sold out online, some additional tickets will be available to purchase at the door on a first come, first served basis on the night of the show. Box office & doors open approximately 1 hour before showtime. For shows that have sold out online, in-person ticket purchases are limited to 2 tickets per person. Paul Sheldon used to write for a living. Now, he’s writing to stay alive. When a famed novelist crashes his car during a blinding blizzard, he wakes to find himself at the mercy of his number one fan. Rob Reiner helms one of the best book-to-screen Stephen King adaptations, a delightfully cunning cat-and-mouse horror thriller fueled by a darkly comedic script from William Goldman and standout performances by James Caan and Academy Award winner Kathy Bates. “The actors are supported by the best kind of writerly craft and directorial technique, the kind that refuses to call attention to itself, never gets caught straining for scares or laughs. Popular moviemaking — elegantly economical, artlessly artful – doesn’t get much better than this.” – Richard Schickel, TIME “The movie has a real kick to it. As Paul and Annie attempt to outsmart each other, Misery gets nastier and nastier. It turns into a psychotic cat-and-mouse game, and there are some genuine shocks.” – Owen Gleiberman, Entertainment Weekly Just added: Dead Calm director Phillip Noyce and star Billy Zane, plus Sleeping Dogs director Roger Donaldson, will join us in person, schedules permitting, to pay tribute to their colleague & friend Sam Neill before their film on Friday, July 24th. When a veteran sailor and his wife rescue the half-delirious sole survivor of a crippled schooner on a dead-calm ocean, the skeptical husband rows to the disabled boat and finds a horrifying sight. Turning back to his own ship, he sees her racing away – with his wife on board – starting a desperate cat-and-mouse game on the high seas. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: Rob Reiner | Writer: Screenplay by William Goldman based on the novel by Stephen King | Starring: James Caan, Kathy Bates, Frances Sternhagen, Richard Farnsworth, Lauren Bacall | Year: 1990 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Phillip Noyce | Writer: Screenplay by Terry Hayes based on the novel by Charles Williams | Starring: Sam Neill, Nicole Kidman, Billy Zane | Year: 1989 | Country: Australia | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Fri July 24 - 6:00 pm / 8:15 pm

Jackson, Uma Thurman, Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Amanda Plummer, Maria de Medeiros, Ving Rhames, Eric Stoltz, Rosanna Arquette, Christopher Walken and Bruce Willis | Year: 1994 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 154 minuti

Orari: Fri August 07 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The War of the Worlds (1953) / Earth vs. the Flying Saucers
item.description:
H.G. Wells’ most famous and fantastic story rockets across the screen in one of the most celebrated science fiction films of all time! When a mysterious object lands in a small Southern California town, a Martian invasion unleashes a mighty panorama of earth-shaking fury. Genre icon producer George Pal presents a classic terror tale for the atomic age, a thrill-a-minute stunner featuring Oscar-winning visual effects, iconic leads in Gene Barry and Ann Robinson, a stellar Leith Stevens score, and costuming by Edith Head. Relive the exciting days of sci-fi movie matinees with the cult classic Earth vs. the Flying Saucers ! Featuring extraordinary visual effects by cinematic genius Ray Harryhausen, the film pits earthlings against alien humanoids in a violent battle for Earth’s survival! When the zombielike aliens arrive at the U.S. Army base in search of help for their dying planet, they try to make friendly contact with scientist Dr. Russ Marvin (Hugh Marlowe) and his recent bride Carol (Joan Taylor). But the military greets their fleet of saucers with gunfire, and the aliens are forced to retaliate. Can Marvin invent the ultimate weapon in a deadly game of beat-the-clock to save the human race? (Sony Pictures)
Cast: Director: Byron Haskin | Writer: Screenplay by Barré Lyndon based on the novel by H.G. Wells | Starring: Gene Barry, Ann Robinson, Les Tremayne, Robert Cornthwaite | Year: 1953 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Fred F. Sears | Writer: Screenplay by Bernard Gordon & George Worthing Yates, screen story by Curt Siodmak, supported by "Flying Saucers from Outer Space" by Major Donald E. Keyhoe | Starring: Hugh Marlowe, Joan Taylor, Donald Curtis, Morris Ankrum | Year: 1956 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 83 minuti

Orari: Sun August 23 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

item.title: These Are the Damned / The Curse of the Werewolf
item.description:
Hammer Film Productions creates an icily strange juvenile delinquent / sci-fi horror hybrid featuring an unforgettable Oliver Reed! In England, a young American falls in love with the sister of a sadistic and lecherous motorcycle gang leader who despises the American. The couple takes refuge from the gang leader’s harassment in a cave where they discover a group of children, who are the result of an experiment by a scientist seeking to develop a race of humans capable of surviving an atomic blast. (Sony Pictures) Oliver Reed stars in a lavish horror fave from Hammer Films! Half-man… half-wolf… compelled by the hideous curse of his evil birth to destroy even those who loved him! This maniacal tale of terror follows Reed, the orphaned baby of a maniacal beggar and a mute girl, from birth to manhood, when he discovers his horrible secret. The cursed youth is unable to suppress the dark forces within him and when the moon is full, he becomes an uncontrollable killer incapable of distinguishing between friend and foe. (Universal Studios)
Cast: Director: Joseph Losey | Writer: Screenplay by Evan Jones based on the novel "The Children of Light" by H.L. Lawrence | Starring: Macdonald Carey, Shirley Anne Field, Viveca Lindfors, Oliver Reed, Alexander Knox | Year: 1962 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm | Director: Terence Fisher | Writer: Screenplay by John Elder [Anthony Hinds] from the novel "The Werewolf of Paris" by Guy Endore | Starring: Clifford Evans, Oliver Reed, Yvonne Romain, Catherine Feller | Year: 1961 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm

item.title: Knights of the Round Table
item.description:
All the glory and splendor of King Arthur’s court presented in massive CinemaScope! The Arthurian legend receives a lavish Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer adaptation, lusciously lensed by Freddie Young ( Lawrence of Arabia ) & Stephen Dade ( Zulu ), and featuring a stirring Miklos Rozsa score. Sir Lancelot (Robert Taylor) is sworn to serve King Arthur (Mel Ferrer), but his devotion to the queen (Ava Gardner) could provide fuel for their enemies to destroy Camelot.
Cast: Director: Richard Thorpe | Writer: Screen play by Talbot Jennings & Jan Lustig and Noel Langley | Starring: Robert Taylor, Ava Gardner, Mel Ferrer, Anne Crawford, Stanley Baker, Felix Aylmer, Maureen Swanson | Year: 1953 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 115 minuti

Orari: Sat August 15 - 2:00 pm

item.title: The Mummy (1999)
item.description:
The sands will rise. The heavens will part. The power will be unleashed. Brendan Fraser & Rachel Weisz star in a rollicking, thrill-a-minute adventure that’s one of the most entertaining blockbusters of the ‘90s. When an American adventurer (Fraser) teams with an Egyptologist librarian (Weisz) and her brother (John Hannah) to uncover the lost City of the Dead, they’ll discover that disturbing the slumber of an ancient priest doomed for eternity could destroy the world.
Cast: Director: Stephen Sommers | Writer: Screenplay by Stephen Sommers, Screen story by Stephen Sommers and Lloyd Fonvielle & Kevin Jarre | Starring: Brendan Fraser, Rachel Weisz, John Hannah, Arnold Vosloo, Kevin J. O'Connor, Jonathan Hyde, Oded Fehr | Year: 1999 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 125 minuti

Orari: Sat August 22 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Dusty and Sweets McGee (IB Tech Print) / Born to Win
item.description:
Meet Tip, Pam, Larry, Kit, Dusty, Sweets McGee and nameless, aimless more with young faces and old eyes. Meet them, but don’t get too attached. Because tomorrow some of them may not be alive. Most of the people who appear in his astonishing semi-documentary about the daily life of Los Angeles heroin abusers are real-life addicts, caught up in an endless cycle of stealing, hustling, jail time, shooting up, and coming down. Realistic and unapologetic, the film includes only a handful of actors, including Billy Grey ( Father Knows Best ) portraying a street-smart drug dealer. Directed by Floyd Mutrux ( American Hot Wax ), with graphic cinematography by William A. Fraker ( Bullitt ), Dusty and Sweets McGee is “a film in its own category. There are long stretches during which it’s hard to take your eyes off the screen” (Mick LaSalle, San Francisco Chronicle ). (Warner Bros) A Times Square junkie who dreams of freedom and dignity gets between a pusher and two narcs out to burn the dealer. (Park Circus) George Segal headlines an unforgettable document of ‘70s New York City. Director Ivan Passer’s first English-language film features a stellar supporting cast that includes Karen Black, Paula Prentiss, Robert De Niro, and more!
Cast: Director: Floyd Mutrux | Writer: Floyd Mutrux | Starring: Clifton Tip Fredell, Kit Ryder, Billy Gray | Year: 1971 | Country: USA | Format: I.B. Technicolor 35mm | Director: Ivan Passer | Writer: Screenplay by David Scott Milton & Ivan Passer, original story by David Scott Milton | Starring: George Segal, Paula Prentiss, Karen Black, Jay Fletcher, Hector Elizondo, Robert De Niro | Year: 1971 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 89 minuti

Orari: Wed July 29 - 7:30 pm / 9:30 pm

item.title: The War of the Worlds (1953) / Earth vs. the Flying Saucers
item.description:
H.G. Wells’ most famous and fantastic story rockets across the screen in one of the most celebrated science fiction films of all time! When a mysterious object lands in a small Southern California town, a Martian invasion unleashes a mighty panorama of earth-shaking fury. Genre icon producer George Pal presents a classic terror tale for the atomic age, a thrill-a-minute stunner featuring Oscar-winning visual effects, iconic leads in Gene Barry and Ann Robinson, a stellar Leith Stevens score, and costuming by Edith Head. Relive the exciting days of sci-fi movie matinees with the cult classic Earth vs. the Flying Saucers ! Featuring extraordinary visual effects by cinematic genius Ray Harryhausen, the film pits earthlings against alien humanoids in a violent battle for Earth’s survival! When the zombielike aliens arrive at the U.S. Army base in search of help for their dying planet, they try to make friendly contact with scientist Dr. Russ Marvin (Hugh Marlowe) and his recent bride Carol (Joan Taylor). But the military greets their fleet of saucers with gunfire, and the aliens are forced to retaliate. Can Marvin invent the ultimate weapon in a deadly game of beat-the-clock to save the human race? (Sony Pictures)
Cast: Director: Byron Haskin | Writer: Screenplay by Barré Lyndon based on the novel by H.G. Wells | Starring: Gene Barry, Ann Robinson, Les Tremayne, Robert Cornthwaite | Year: 1953 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Fred F. Sears | Writer: Screenplay by Bernard Gordon & George Worthing Yates, screen story by Curt Siodmak, supported by "Flying Saucers from Outer Space" by Major Donald E. Keyhoe | Starring: Hugh Marlowe, Joan Taylor, Donald Curtis, Morris Ankrum | Year: 1956 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 83 minuti

Orari: Sat August 22 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

item.title: Taxi Driver
item.description:
Martin Scorsese directs Paul Schrader’s searing script about alienation and desperation, following a shattered nightcrawler haunting the neon-lit streets of seedy ‘70s NYC as a nightshift Taxi Driver as he hurtles towards his imagined destiny. The gritty and hard-hitting masterpiece features an all-time great performance from Robert De Niro and scored the Palme d’Or at the Cannes Film Festival for the director. “No other film has ever dramatized urban indifference so powerfully; at first, here, it’s horrifyingly funny, and then just horrifying.” – Pauline Kael, New Yorker “Martin Scorsese’s 1976 film [ Taxi Driver ] is a film that does not grow dated, or over-familiar. I have seen it dozens of times. Every time I see it, it works; I am drawn into Travis’ underworld of alienation, loneliness, haplessness and anger.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Martin Scorsese | Writer: Paul Schrader | Starring: Robert De Niro, Jodie Foster, Albert Brooks, Harvey Keitel, Leonard Harris, Peter Boyle, Cybill Shepherd | Year: 1976 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 114 minuti

Orari: Sun August 09 - 6:30 pm

item.title: Pulp Fiction
item.description:
Two hitmen out to retrieve a suitcase stolen. An aging boxer paid to lose his next fight. A couple of young lovers and small-time thieves who decide they need a change of venue. Their lives are woven together in the exhilarating Pulp Fiction . Quentin Tarantino’s Palme d’Or-winning classic is packed with electric dialogue, potent pop-culture touchstones, and an unforgettable ensemble cast. You won’t know the facts until you’ve seen the fiction. “A spectacularly entertaining piece of pop culture, Pulp Fiction is the American Graffiti of violent crime pictures.” – Todd McCarthy, Variety “ Pulp Fiction crackles like an ozone generator: Here’s a director who’s been let loose inside the toy store, and wants to play all night. 4 Stars ” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Written by Quentin Tarantino, Stories by Quentin Tarantino & Roger Avary | Starring: John Travolta, Samuel L. Jackson, Uma Thurman, Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Amanda Plummer, Maria de Medeiros, Ving Rhames, Eric Stoltz, Rosanna Arquette, Christopher Walken and Bruce Willis | Year: 1994 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 154 minuti

Orari: Fri August 14 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The Informer (1935) / Air Mail
item.description:
Gypo Nolan stumbles through a  foggy Irish night, his brain pickled in whiskey and his soul tormented by shame and fear. Gypo is an informer, a turncoat who betrayed his friend to the British police for 20 pounds. Now he can’t spend the money fast enough – nor can he run from his treachery fast enough – as he brawls, brags, swaggers and lies his way toward his fate. John Ford earned his first Best Director Academy Award, and star Victor McLaglen took home a Best Actor statuette for this searing four-time Oscar winner set in 1922 Dublin. Timely in its exploration of the tortured netherworld of human guilt, The Informer is filmmaking for the ages. (Warner Bros) Neither snow, nor rain, nor heat, nor gloom of night stays these couriers from the swift completion of their appointed rounds. Seven years before Howard Hawks’ Only Angels Have Wings , director John Ford helms a tale following a tight-knit group of air mail pilots risking their lives through dangerous weather and over treacherous terrain.  When a flyer is killed in a fiery crash, the head aviator (Ralph Bellamy) will be forced to hire a reckless daredevil (Pat O’Brien) as a replacement.
Cast: Director: John Ford | Writer: Screen play by Dudley Nichols from the story by Liam O'Flaherty | Starring: Victor McLaglen, Heather Angel, Preston Foster, Margot Grahame, Wallace Ford, Una O'Connor | Year: 1935 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: John Ford | Starring: Ralph Bellamy, Gloria Stuart, Pat O'Brien, Slim Summerville, Lilian Bond | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 84 minuti

Orari: Mon August 03 - 7:30 pm / 9:30 pm

item.description:
Martin Scorsese directs Paul Schrader’s searing script about alienation and desperation, following a shattered nightcrawler haunting the neon-lit streets of seedy ‘70s NYC as a nightshift Taxi Driver as he hurtles towards his imagined destiny. item.title: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie
item.description:
While our allotment of advance tickets has sold out online, some additional tickets will be available to purchase at the door on a first come, first served basis on the night of the show. For shows that have sold out online, in-person ticket purchases are limited to 2 tickets per person. The cult Canadian show makes the giant leap to the big screen with this wildly ambitious & riotously funny instant-classic that demands to be seen with an audience. When their elaborate plans to book a show at the Rivoli goes horribly wrong, a pair of best buds (Matt Johnson & Jay McCarrol) accidentally get sent back in time to the year 2008.
Cast: Director: Matt Johnson | Starring: Matt Johnson, Jay McCarrol | Year: 2025 | Country: Canada | Format: 35mm

item.title: Whisper of the Heart (English Dubbed Version)
item.description:
An animated coming of age classic from the beloved Studio Ghibli, featuring a funny, heartfelt screenplay by Hayao Miyazaki based on the original manga by Aoi Hiiragi. When a quiet, creative girl with dreams of becoming a writer looks carefully through her library books, she discovers they were all checked out previously by the same person, leading her on a life-changing quest that unlocks her talent & potential. Take Me Home, Country Roads.
Cast: Director: Yoshifumi Kondô | Year: 1995 | Country: Japan | Format: English dubbed 35mm
Durata: 111 minuti

Orari: Sat August 01 - 2:00 pm

item.title: My Dinner with Andre / Vanya on 42nd Street
item.description:
In this captivating and philosophical film directed by Louis Malle, actor and playwright Wallace Shawn sits down with his friend the theater director André Gregory at a restaurant on New York’s Upper West Side, and the pair proceed through an alternately whimsical and despairing confessional about love, death, money, and all the superstition in between. Playing variations on their own New York–honed personas, Shawn and Gregory, who also cowrote the screenplay, dive in with introspective intellectual gusto, and Malle captures it all with a delicate, artful detachment. A fascinating freeze-frame of cosmopolitan culture, My Dinner with André remains a unique work in cinema history. (Janus Films) The collaborators of the acclaimed My Dinner with Andre (Louis Malle, Andre Gregory and Wallace Shawn) re-team for this update of Anton Checkhov’s Uncle Vanya, a timeless drama of faith, hope and the redemptive powers of love. Shawn and co-star Julianne Moore head the extraordinary ensemble cast in playwright David Mamet’s striking new adaptation of Checkhov’s drama of people trapped between the harsh reality of their lives and the longings of their hearts. Buffeted by a jaunty jazz score from Grammy nominee Joshua Redman, “this live-wire Vanya, freshly observed for the ‘90s is fiercely funny, touching and vital!” – Peter Travers, Rolling Stone (Sony Pictures Classics)
Cast: Director: Louis Malle | Writer: Screenplay by Wallace Shawn & Andre Gregory | Starring: Andre Gregory, Wallace Shawn | Year: 1981 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Louis Malle | Writer: From André Gregory's "Vanya" based on Anton Chekhov's "Uncle Vanya" adapted by David Mamet | Starring: Larry Pine, Brooke Smith, Wallace Shawn, André Gregory, Julianne Moore | Year: 1994 | Country: UK/USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 119 minuti

item.title: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea (1916)(Silent with Live Music Score)
item.description:
Featuring live musical accompaniment by Cliff Retallick A groundbreaking underwater adventure based on the timeless Jules Verne tale! When a vessel investigating a monster terrorizing the seas is attacked, they’ll discover Captain Nemo and his fantastic submarine, the Nautilus.  But the danger & mysteries deepen as Nemo’s backstory comes into focus.

item.title: Misery / Dead Calm
item.description:
Paul Sheldon used to write for a living. Now, he’s writing to stay alive. When a famed novelist crashes his car during a blinding blizzard, he wakes to find himself at the mercy of his number one fan. Rob Reiner helms one of the best book-to-screen Stephen King adaptations, a delightfully cunning cat-and-mouse horror thriller fueled by a darkly comedic script from William Goldman and standout performances by James Caan and Academy Award winner Kathy Bates. “The actors are supported by the best kind of writerly craft and directorial technique, the kind that refuses to call attention to itself, never gets caught straining for scares or laughs. Popular moviemaking — elegantly economical, artlessly artful – doesn’t get much better than this.” – Richard Schickel, TIME “The movie has a real kick to it. As Paul and Annie attempt to outsmart each other, Misery gets nastier and nastier. It turns into a psychotic cat-and-mouse game, and there are some genuine shocks.” – Owen Gleiberman, Entertainment Weekly Sam Neill, Nicole Kidman, and Billy Zane star in an edge-of-your-seat thriller! When a veteran sailor and his wife rescue the half-delirious sole survivor of a crippled schooner on a dead-calm ocean, the skeptical husband rows to the disabled boat and finds a horrifying sight. Turning back to his own ship, he sees her racing away – with his wife on board – starting a desperate cat-and-mouse game on the high seas. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: Rob Reiner | Writer: Screenplay by William Goldman based on the novel by Stephen King | Starring: James Caan, Kathy Bates, Frances Sternhagen, Richard Farnsworth, Lauren Bacall | Year: 1990 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Phillip Noyce | Writer: Screenplay by Terry Hayes based on the novel by Charles Williams | Starring: Sam Neill, Nicole Kidman, Billy Zane | Year: 1989 | Country: Australia | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Sun July 26 - 6:30 pm / 8:45 pm

item.title: Jackie Brown
item.description:
A stewardess, a gun runner, a bail bondsman, a federal agent, an ex-con and a stoned-out beach bunny are all on the trail of half-million dollars in Quentin Tarantino’s Jackie Brown , a mesmerizing, all-star adaptation of Elmore Leonard’s novel Rum Punch . But who’s getting played and who’s gonna make the big score? “This is the movie that proves Tarantino is the real thing, and not just a two-film wonder boy. It’s not a retread of Reservoir Dogs or Pulp Fiction , but a new film in a new style, and it evokes the particular magic of Elmore Leonard – who elevates the crime novel to a form of sociological comedy. 4 Stars.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Pam Grier, Robert Forster, Samuel L. Jackson, Bridget Fonda, Michael Keaton, Robert De Niro, Michael Bowen, Chris Tucker | Year: 1997 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 154 minuti

Orari: Fri July 24 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Jackie Brown
item.description:
A stewardess, a gun runner, a bail bondsman, a federal agent, an ex-con and a stoned-out beach bunny are all on the trail of half-million dollars in Quentin Tarantino’s Jackie Brown , a mesmerizing, all-star adaptation of Elmore Leonard’s novel Rum Punch . But who’s getting played and who’s gonna make the big score? “This is the movie that proves Tarantino is the real thing, and not just a two-film wonder boy. It’s not a retread of Reservoir Dogs or Pulp Fiction , but a new film in a new style, and it evokes the particular magic of Elmore Leonard – who elevates the crime novel to a form of sociological comedy. 4 Stars.” – Roger Ebert

Orari: Fri July 31 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The Blade / Love In The Time Of Twilight
item.description:
Director Tsui Hark helms one of the most kinetic & visually striking action films to emerge from the Hong Kong golden age! A young man discovers the truth about his father’s death and embarks on quest for revenge where he loses his right arm and is left for dead. But when violence comes to his home again, he’ll have to pick up his father’s broken sword and find a new way to wield it. “Tsui Hark deconstructs the world of chivalrous heroes, turning it into a man-eat-dog hellscape where mercy is just another word for ‘weakness.’ Reimagining Chang Cheh’s landmark 1967 film, The One-Armed Swordsmen , as a psychotronic phantasmagoria full of scars and tattoos, mutilation, amputation, sexual frustration and heavy chunks of steel-splitting muscle and breaking bones, Tsui rolls his superstylized camera through the dirt and turns the freeze-frame into a tombstone.” – Gray Hendrix, Time Out Director Tsui Hark unleashed three films in 1995, including his violent masterpiece The Blade . Here he helms what at first seems like a simple period romance but quickly morphs into a truly wild tale of ghostly spirits, bank robbery, time travel, lamp posts & light bulbs, vomit jokes, and ‘90s cgi. You’ve never seen anything like it!
Cast: Director: Tsui Hark | Starring: Vincent Zhao, Xiong Xinxin, Song Lei, Austin Wai and Moses Chan | Year: 1995 | Country: Hong Kong | Format: English subtitled 35mm | Director: Tsui Hark | Starring: Nicky Wu, Charlie Yeung, Eric Kot, William Ho | Year: 1995 | Country: Hong Kong | Format: English subtitled 35mm
Durata: 103 minuti

Orari: Mon August 31 - 7:30 pm / 9:45 pm

item.title: Back to the Future
item.description:
Marty McFly’s having the time of his life. The only question is what time is it? When an ‘80s high schooler is accidentally sent to the year 1955, he’ll have to find a way back without destroying his future. Michael J. Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson, and Crispin Glover star in the sci-fi comedy blockbuster from director Robert Zemeckis and producer Steven Spielberg.
Cast: Director: Robert Zemeckis | Writer: Robert Zemeckis & Bob Gale | Starring: Michael J. Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson, Crispin Glover, Thomas F. Wilson, Claudia Wells, Marc McClure, Wendie Jo Sperber | Year: 1985 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 116 minuti

Orari: Sat July 25 - 2:00 pm

item.title: To Live and Die in L.A. / The Rip
item.description:
A federal agent is dead. A killer is loose. And the City of Angels is about to explode. In one of the slickest thrillers ever to hit the streets of Los Angeles, a Secret Service agent ( C.S.I. ‘s William Petersen) vows to do whatever it takes in search of the counterfeiter (Willem Dafoe) who killed his partner. To Live and Die in L.A. is razor sharp and exquisitely stylized, William Friedkin’s West Coast mic drop on mid-80s Miami Vice mania, featuring a pulsing score by Wang Chung and sizzling cinematography by Robby Muller. Every scene feels authentic and dangerously alive; the film was scripted by an actual Secret Service agent and ex-cons were consulted to make sure the counterfeiting was done right. Plus, it’s got one of the all-time great car chases and an incendiary finale that will leave you breathless! “William Friedkin was making sleek, sensational, ice-cold thrillers long before rock videos and Miami Vice were fashionable. And he couldn’t be more in his element now that they are. To Live and Die in L.A. is Mr. Friedkin at his glossiest, a great-looking, riveting movie” – Janet Maslin, The New York Times “In the hierarchy of great movie chase sequences, the recent landmarks include the chases under the Brooklyn elevated tracks in The French Connection and down the hills of San Francisco in Bullitt … [ To Live and Die In L.A. ] contains another chase that belongs on that short list. 4 Stars! ” – Roger Ebert “An exciting cop thriller with a novel premise that manages to deliver the goods in really clever ways. The whole package worked for me” – Quentin Tarantino Trust frays when a team of Miami cops discovers millions in cash inside a run-down stash house, calling everyone – and everything – into question. (Netflix)
Cast: Director: William Friedkin | Writer: screenplay by William Friedkin & Gerald Petievich based on the novel by Gerald Petievich | Starring: William Petersen, Willem Dafoe, John Pankow, Debra Feuer, John Turturro, Darlanne Fluegel, Dean Stockwell, Steve James, Robert Downey Sr | Year: 1985 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe Carnahan | Writer: Screenplay by Joe Carnahan, story by Joe Carnahan & Michael McGrale | Starring: Matt Damon, Ben Affleck, Steven Yeun, Teyana Taylor, Catalina Sandino Moreno, Sasha Calle, Kyle Chandler, Scott Adkins | Year: 2026 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 113 minuti

Orari: Thu August 27 - 7:30 pm / 9:55 pm

item.title: Tiger on the Beat 2 / The Cyprus Tigers
item.description:
This in-name-only sequel to the first film – but peppered with much of the same cast – is an action-packed Hong Kong cop comedy. Captain Lam is nearing 40 and looking to accomplish something memorable before retiring. But first he’s gotta help find his nephew a suitable wife… while avoiding the deadly crosshairs of a dangerous smuggling ring. The Killer ’s Danny Lee headlines with martial arts superstar Conan Lee. A trio of undercover Chinese cops in the beautiful Mediterranean take on a counterfeiting ring in this rarely screened blast of bonkers cinema. Simon Yam, Collin Cheung & Conan Lee star as The Cyprus Tigers , while the unique locale provides an interesting backdrop for a Hong Kong action comedy.
Cast: Director: Lau Kar-Leung | Starring: Danny Lee, Conan Lee, Ellen Chan, Roy Cheung | Year: 1990 | Country: Hong Kong | Format: English subtitled 35mm | Director: Phillip Ko | Starring: Simon Yam, Conan Lee, Collin Cheung, Robin Shou, Joey Wang | Year: 1990 | Country: Hong Kong | Format: English subtitled 35mm
Durata: 91 minuti

Orari: Mon July 27 - 7:30 pm / 9:35 pm

item.title: School of Rock
item.description:
In the role he was born to play, Jack Black is Dewey Finn, a slacker guitar player still holding on to the dream of being a rock star. Due to a mix-up (and a low-level con), the wildly unqualified Dewey ends up substitute teacher in a prestigious elementary school. He recruits the bored prep school kids to form a new band, so these talented kids can rock the battle of the bands like it’s never been rocked before. This hilariously quoteable contemporary classic made Jack Black a superstar. And of course, it’s got a killer rock n rock soundtrack featuring songs from Led Zeppelin, Cream, T. Rex and the Black Keys, not to mention the original anthems performed by the kids themselves. Let’s get rockin! “If quirky, independent, grown-up outsider filmmakers set out to make a family movie, this is the kind of movie they would make. And they did.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Richard Linklater | Writer: Mike White | Starring: Jack Black, Joan Cusack, Mike White, Sarah Silverman, Miranda Cosgrove | Year: 2003 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 109 minuti

Orari: Sun August 09 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Ball of Fire / The Lady Eve
item.description:
Gary Cooper & Barbara Stanwyck star in a comedy classic from director Howard Hawks and screenwriters Charles Brackett and Billy Wilder! On the run, a burlesque dancer hides out in a house where eight stuffy professors are compiling an encyclopaedia on current American slang… and gets taken on as an expert in “slanguage.” (Park Circus) The perfect date night delight! Preston Sturges expertly mixes superb screwball situations and gut-busting giggles with sweet-natured romance and some of the best on-screen chemistry ever captured on film. After a year-long expedition up the Amazon, a naive heir to a brewery fortune (Henry Fonda) finds himself aboard a luxury liner and in the sites of a trio of sharp-witted card sharps. But fate has other plans when the beautiful con artist (Barbara Stanwyck) begins to fall for her mark.
Cast: Director: Howard Hawks | Writer: Screen play by Charles Brackett and Billy Wilder from an original story by Billy Wilder and Thomas Monroe | Starring: Gary Cooper, Barbara Stanwyck | Year: 1941 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Preston Sturges | Writer: Preston Sturges based on a story by Monckton Hoffe | Starring: Barbara Stanwyck, Henry Fonda, Charles Coburn, Eugene Pallette, Martha O'Driscoll, William Demarest, Eric Blore | Year: 1941 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 94 minuti

Orari: Wed August 19 - 7:30 pm / 9:50 pm

item.title: Moneyball
item.description:
Oakland A’s general manager Billy Beane (Brad Pitt) challenges the system and defies conventional wisdom when he is forced to rebuild his small-market team, on a limited budget. Despite opposition from the old guard, the media, fans and their own manager (Philip Seymour Hoffman), Beane – with the help of a young, number-crunching, Yale-educated economist (Jonah Hill) – develops a roster of misfits… and along the way, forever changes the way the game is played. (Sony Pictures)
Cast: Director: Bennett Miller | Writer: Screenplay bySteven Zaillian and Aaron Sorkin, story by Stan Chervin, based on the book by Michael Lewis | Starring: Brad Pitt, Jonah Hill, Philip Seymour Hoffman, Robin Wright, Chris Pratt | Year: 2011 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 133 minuti

Orari: Thu August 20 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Throw Momma from the Train / Ruthless People
item.description:
Danny DeVito and Billy Crystal star in a black comedy riff on Hitchcock’s Strangers on a Train ! Crystal plays a newly divorced sad sack bitter that his ex-wife stole his novel and turned it into her own bestseller. DeVito is the long-suffering momma’s boy eager to get out from his mother’s thumb. But when one suggests trading murders as the perfect alibi, the foul play will spin hilariously out of control. DeVito directs his first theatrical feature film, while director of photography Barry Sonnenfeld adds a stylish & energetic verve to the proceedings (the same year he also helmed Raising Arizona ). Plus, Anne Ramsey is unforgettable as DeVito’s domineering mother. In this fiendishly funny comedy from the creators of Airplane! , loathsome millionaire Sam Stone (Danny DeVito) is RUTHLESS. How ruthless? When his wife Barbara (Bette Midler) is nabbed by inept kidnappers (Judge Reinhold, Helen Slater), Sam cries tears of joy… and refuses to pay the ransom. And when the abductors threaten to kill the abrasive heiress, Sam takes immediate action – he celebrates! Ruthless People . Raucous… outlandish… one of the top box office hits of the year! (Touchstone Pictures)
Cast: Director: Danny DeVito | Writer: Stu Silver | Starring: Danny DeVito, Billy Crystal, Kim Greist, Anne Ramsey | Year: 1987 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Jim Abrahams, David Zucker, Jerry Zucker | Writer: Dale Launer | Starring: Danny DeVito, Judge Reinhold, Helen Slater, Bette Midler | Year: 1986 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm

item.title: Strange Brew
item.description:
Canadian slackers Bob & Doug McKenzie (Rick Moranis & Dave Thomas) will do anything to keep the suds flowing. But when they land a gig at Elsinore Brewing and discover a diabolical scheme to take over the world, they’ll have to stop the evil Brewmeister Smith (Max von Sydow) and his hockey-playing hellions in this SCTV cult classic.
Cast: Director: Rick Moranis, Dave Thomas | Writer: Written by Rick Moranis & Dave Thomas and Steven De Jarnatt | Starring: Dave Thomas, Rick Moranis, Max von Sydow, Paul Dooley, Lynne Griffin | Year: 1983 | Country: Canada/USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 90 minuti

Orari: Sat August 29 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The 39 Steps (1935) / Sabotage (1936)
item.description:
A heart-racing spy story by Alfred Hitchcock, The 39 Steps follows Richard Hannay (Robert Donat) as he stumbles upon a conspiracy that thrusts him into a hectic chase across the Scottish moors – a chase in which he is both the pursuer and the pursued – as well as into an unexpected romance with the cool Pamela (Madeline Carroll). This classic wrong-man thriller from the Master of Suspense anticipates the director’s most famous works (especially North by Northwest ), and remains one of his cleverest and most entertaining films. (American Genre Film Archive) One of the most playful of Hitchcock’s British thrillers, Sabotage doesn’t waste one second of its taut 77-minute runtime, ratcheting up the suspense in pointillistically beautiful ways. Sylvia Sidney is the unsuspecting wife of a London cinema owner (Oskar Homolka) whom a Scotland Yard detective (John Loder) comes to believe is behind a string of terrorist attacks. The gripping centerpiece – involving a boy, a film canister and a ticking time bomb – is one of the most heart-stopping moments of pure suspense in all of Hitchcock’s canon. (American Genre Film Archive)
Cast: Director: Alfred Hitchcock | Starring: Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle, Peggy Ashcroft | Year: 1935 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm | Director: Alfred Hitchcock | Starring: Sylvia Sidney, Oskar Homolka, Desmond Tester, John Loder | Year: 1936 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 76 minuti

Orari: Sat August 29 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

item.title: The Man with the Golden Gun
item.description:
He never misses his target, and now his target is 007. Roger Moore is back as Bond, this time on a dangerous mission to recover a stolen solar invention. But The Man with the Golden Gun , the world’s most expensive assassin, his hunting him . Christopher Lee, Britt Ekland, Maud Adams, and Hervé Villechaize co-star in this globetrotting action hit filmed across Thailand, Hong Kong, Macau, and beyond.
Cast: Director: Guy Hamilton | Writer: Screenplay by Richard Maibaum and Tom Mankiewicz | Starring: Roger Moore, Christopher Lee, Britt Ekland, Maud Adams, Hervé Villechaize, Clifton James | Year: 1974 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 125 minuti

item.title: Dusty and Sweets McGee (IB Tech Print) / Born to Win
item.description:
Meet Tip, Pam, Larry, Kit, Dusty, Sweets McGee and nameless, aimless more with young faces and old eyes. Meet them, but don’t get too attached. Because tomorrow some of them may not be alive. Most of the people who appear in his astonishing semi-documentary about the daily life of Los Angeles heroin abusers are real-life addicts, caught up in an endless cycle of stealing, hustling, jail time, shooting up, and coming down. Realistic and unapologetic, the film includes only a handful of actors, including Billy Grey ( Father Knows Best ) portraying a street-smart drug dealer. Directed by Floyd Mutrux ( American Hot Wax ), with graphic cinematography by William A. Fraker ( Bullitt ), Dusty and Sweets McGee is “a film in its own category. There are long stretches during which it’s hard to take your eyes off the screen” (Mick LaSalle, San Francisco Chronicle ). (Warner Bros) A Times Square junkie who dreams of freedom and dignity gets between a pusher and two narcs out to burn the dealer. (Park Circus) George Segal headlines an unforgettable document of ‘70s New York City. Director Ivan Passer’s first English-language film features a stellar supporting cast that includes Karen Black, Paula Prentiss, Robert De Niro, and more!
Cast: Director: Floyd Mutrux | Writer: Floyd Mutrux | Starring: Clifton Tip Fredell, Kit Ryder, Billy Gray | Year: 1971 | Country: USA | Format: I.B. Technicolor 35mm | Director: Ivan Passer | Writer: Screenplay by David Scott Milton & Ivan Passer, original story by David Scott Milton | Starring: George Segal, Paula Prentiss, Karen Black, Jay Fletcher, Hector Elizondo, Robert De Niro | Year: 1971 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 89 minuti

Orari: Tue July 28 - 7:30 pm / 9:30 pm

item.title: Taxi Driver
item.description:
Martin Scorsese directs Paul Schrader’s searing script about alienation and desperation, following a shattered nightcrawler haunting the neon-lit streets of seedy ‘70s NYC as a nightshift Taxi Driver as he hurtles towards his imagined destiny. The gritty and hard-hitting masterpiece features an all-time great performance from Robert De Niro and scored the Palme d’Or at the Cannes Film Festival for the director. “No other film has ever dramatized urban indifference so powerfully; at first, here, it’s horrifyingly funny, and then just horrifying.” – Pauline Kael, New Yorker “Martin Scorsese’s 1976 film [ Taxi Driver ] is a film that does not grow dated, or over-familiar. I have seen it dozens of times. Every time I see it, it works; I am drawn into Travis’ underworld of alienation, loneliness, haplessness and anger.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Martin Scorsese | Writer: Paul Schrader | Starring: Robert De Niro, Jodie Foster, Albert Brooks, Harvey Keitel, Leonard Harris, Peter Boyle, Cybill Shepherd | Year: 1976 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 114 minuti

Orari: Sat August 08 - 7:30 pm

item.title: The Mummy (1999)
item.description:
The sands will rise. The heavens will part. The power will be unleashed. Brendan Fraser & Rachel Weisz star in a rollicking, thrill-a-minute adventure that’s one of the most entertaining blockbusters of the ‘90s. When an American adventurer (Fraser) teams with an Egyptologist librarian (Weisz) and her brother (John Hannah) to uncover the lost City of the Dead, they’ll discover that disturbing the slumber of an ancient priest doomed for eternity could destroy the world.
Cast: Director: Stephen Sommers | Writer: Screenplay by Stephen Sommers, Screen story by Stephen Sommers and Lloyd Fonvielle & Kevin Jarre | Starring: Brendan Fraser, Rachel Weisz, John Hannah, Arnold Vosloo, Kevin J. O'Connor, Jonathan Hyde, Oded Fehr | Year: 1999 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 125 minuti

Orari: Sun August 23 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Dazed and Confused
item.description:
The breakthrough hit for director Richard Linklater , Dazed and Confused is perhaps the greatest hangout movie of all time. A coming-of-age ensemble comedy set on the last day of school 1976, the film manages to define the timeless experience of high school itself. Featuring early roles for Matthew McConaughey, Ben Affleck, and Parker Posey, this stoner timepiece remains totally irresistible and a fitting tribute to the worst / greatest years of your life.
Cast: Director: Richard Linklater | Starring: Jason London, Rory Cochrane, Wiley Wiggins, Adam Goldberg, Matthew McConaughey, Cole Hauser, Parker Posey, Milla Jovovich, Ben Affleck | Year: 1993 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 102 minuti

Orari: Sat July 25 - 11:59 pm

item.title: To Live and Die in L.A. / The Rip
item.description:
A federal agent is dead. A killer is loose. And the City of Angels is about to explode. In one of the slickest thrillers ever to hit the streets of Los Angeles, a Secret Service agent ( C.S.I. ‘s William Petersen) vows to do whatever it takes in search of the counterfeiter (Willem Dafoe) who killed his partner. To Live and Die in L.A. is razor sharp and exquisitely stylized, William Friedkin’s West Coast mic drop on mid-80s Miami Vice mania, featuring a pulsing score by Wang Chung and sizzling cinematography by Robby Muller. Every scene feels authentic and dangerously alive; the film was scripted by an actual Secret Service agent and ex-cons were consulted to make sure the counterfeiting was done right. Plus, it’s got one of the all-time great car chases and an incendiary finale that will leave you breathless! “William Friedkin was making sleek, sensational, ice-cold thrillers long before rock videos and Miami Vice were fashionable. And he couldn’t be more in his element now that they are. To Live and Die in L.A. is Mr. Friedkin at his glossiest, a great-looking, riveting movie” – Janet Maslin, The New York Times “In the hierarchy of great movie chase sequences, the recent landmarks include the chases under the Brooklyn elevated tracks in The French Connection and down the hills of San Francisco in Bullitt … [ To Live and Die In L.A. ] contains another chase that belongs on that short list. 4 Stars! ” – Roger Ebert “An exciting cop thriller with a novel premise that manages to deliver the goods in really clever ways. The whole package worked for me” – Quentin Tarantino Trust frays when a team of Miami cops discovers millions in cash inside a run-down stash house, calling everyone – and everything – into question. (Netflix)
Cast: Director: William Friedkin | Writer: screenplay by William Friedkin & Gerald Petievich based on the novel by Gerald Petievich | Starring: William Petersen, Willem Dafoe, John Pankow, Debra Feuer, John Turturro, Darlanne Fluegel, Dean Stockwell, Steve James, Robert Downey Sr | Year: 1985 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe Carnahan | Writer: Screenplay by Joe Carnahan, story by Joe Carnahan & Michael McGrale | Starring: Matt Damon, Ben Affleck, Steven Yeun, Teyana Taylor, Catalina Sandino Moreno, Sasha Calle, Kyle Chandler, Scott Adkins | Year: 2026 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 113 minuti

Orari: Wed August 26 - 7:30 pm / 9:55 pm

item.description:

The gritty and hard-hitting masterpiece features an all-time great performance from Robert De Niro and scored the Palme d’Or at the Cannes Film Festival for the director. “No other film has ever dramatized urban indifference so powerfully; at first, here, it’s horrifyingly funny, and then just horrifying.” – Pauline Kael, New Yorker “Martin Scorsese’s 1976 film [ Taxi Driver ] is a film that does not grow dated, or over-familiar. Orari: Thu August 06 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Midnight Run / 48 Hrs.
item.description:
The odd couple classic! Robert De Niro is a tough ex-cop tasked with finding Charles Grodin, a mild-mannered mob accountant who’s skipped bail and on the lam. But what was supposed to be a simple Midnight Run turns into a comedic, action-packed cross country chase where he’ll have to get the FBI off his case, the mob off his trail, and Grodin off his back! “ Midnight Run shows that Robert De Niro can be as wonderful in a comic role as he is in a serious one. Pair him, a gruff ex-cop and bounty hunter, with straight man Charles Grodin, his captive, and the result is one of the most entertaining, best executed, original road pictures ever.” – Variety “Whoever cast De Niro and Grodin must have had a sixth sense for the chemistry they would have; they work together so smoothly, and with such an evident sense of fun, that even their silences are intriguing.” – Roger Ebert The boys are back in town. Nick Nolte is a cop. Eddie Murphy is a convict. They couldn’t have liked each other less… They couldn’t have needed each other more. And the last place they every expected to be is on the same side. Even for 48 Hrs. Walter Hill’s exhilarating action comedy is one of the best buddy team-ups of the decade, with Murphy positively electric in his film debut.
Cast: Director: Martin Brest | Writer: George Gallo | Starring: Robert De Niro, Charles Grodin, Yaphet Kotto, John Ashton, Dennis Farina, Joe Pantoliano | Year: 1988 | Director: Walter Hill | Writer: Roger Spottiswoode and Walter Hill & Larry Gross and Steven E. de Souza | Starring: Nick Nolte, Eddie Murphy, Annette O'Toole, Frank McRae, James Remar, David Patrick Kelly, Sonny Landham, Brion James | Year: 1982 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Tue August 04 - 7:30 pm / 10:05 pm

item.title: The Deer Hunter
item.description:
With only one previous effort under his belt (the crime action pic Thunderbolt and Lightfoot (1974), filmmaker Michael Cimino exploded on the scene with this epic three hour war drama, a huge box office success that won 5 Oscars, including Best Picture, Best Supporting Actor (Christopher Walken) and Best Director (Cimino). Scuttling the conventional wisdom that audiences didn’t want to see movies about the Vietnam War, it opened the floodgates for Coppola’s Apocalypse Now (1979), Oliver Stone’s Platoon (1986) and Kubrick’s Full Metal Jacket (1987). Three Russian American Pennsylvania steelworkers (Robert De Niro, John Savage, Walken) celebrate on the eve of their departure for the Army. Once in Nam, the three are thrown into hell, captured by the Viet Cong. Forced to play Russian Roulette by their captors, they turn the tables, escape but are separated. Back home, De Niro must deal with his conflicted feelings about the girlfriend (Meryl Streep) of Walken, and decides to return to Nam after the military pullout to find his traumatized buddy. “A big, awkward, crazily ambitious motion picture that comes as close to being a popular epic as any movie about this country since The Godfather . Its vision is that of an original, major new filmmaker.” – Vincent Canby, The New York Times “A remarkably full screen treatment of the mystic bond of male comradeship… it’s an astonishing piece of work, an uneasy mixture of violent pulp and grandiosity, with an enraptured view of common life – poetry of the commonplace.” – Pauline Kael, New Yorker “ The Deer Hunter is said to be about many subjects: About male bonding, about mindless patriotism, about the dehumanizing effects of war, about Nixon’s ‘silent majority.’ It is about any of those things that you choose, if you choose, but more than anything else, it is a heartbreakingly effective fictional machine that evokes the agony of the Vietnam time.” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Michael Cimino | Writer: Screenplay by Deric Washburn, Story by Michael Cimino & Deric Washburn and Louis Garfinkle & Quinn K. Redeker | Starring: Robert De Niro, John Cazale, John Savage, Christopher Walken, Meryl Streep, George Dzundza | Year: 1978 | Country: USA/UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 184 minuti

Orari: Fri July 31 - 6:30 pm

item.title: Ice Cream Man
item.description:
An idyllic summer town descends into madness when an ice cream man serves kids sweet delights with horrifying results. (The Horror Section)
Cast: Director: Eli Roth | Writer: Screenplay by Eli Roth and Noah Belson, story by Eli Roth | Starring: Ari Millen, Charlie Zeltzer, Shiloh O'Reilly, Kiori Mirza Waldman, Sarah Abbott, Benjamin Byron Davis | Year: 2026 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 86 minuti

item.title: Back to the Future
item.description:
Marty McFly’s having the time of his life. The only question is what time is it? When an ‘80s high schooler is accidentally sent to the year 1955, he’ll have to find a way back without destroying his future. Michael J. Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson, and Crispin Glover star in the sci-fi comedy blockbuster from director Robert Zemeckis and producer Steven Spielberg.
Cast: Director: Robert Zemeckis | Writer: Robert Zemeckis & Bob Gale | Starring: Michael J. Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson, Crispin Glover, Thomas F. Wilson, Claudia Wells, Marc McClure, Wendie Jo Sperber | Year: 1985 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 116 minuti

Orari: Sun July 26 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Ghost World
item.description:
Break out the Blueshammer and green hair dye, Ghost World is coming in 35mm! Documentary filmmaker Terry Zwigoff’s first fictional feature is a bitingly funny adaptation of Daniel Clowes’ famed underground comic. And it’s still as razor sharp as the day it was lensed, a darkly humorous adolescent requiem packed with pitch perfect detail and painfully realistic characters. A sardonic Thora Birch and a young Scarlett Johansson are quintessential outsiders Enid and Rebecca, teenagers adrift in post-high school ennui, suffocated by suburban mediocrity. But crossing paths with sad sack kindred spirit Seymour (Steve Buscemi in the bug-eyed record collector role he was born to play) will change their friendship forever. Filled with a dream supporting cast (Illeana Douglas, Bob Balaban, Brad Renfro, Pat Healy, Teri Garr), Ghost World stands out from the teen-flick pack, creating specific, believable people and all-too-relatable cringe-inducing laughs. Essential viewing for the misfit loner in all of us!
Cast: Director: Terry Zwigoff | Writer: written by Daniel Clowes & Terry Zwigoff based on the comic book by Daniel Clowes | Starring: Thora Birch, Scarlett Johansson, Steve Buscemi, Brad Renfo, Illeana Douglas, Bob Balaban, Teri Garr, Pat Healy | Year: 2001 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 111 minuti

Orari: Sat August 22 - 11:59 pm

item.title: My Dinner with Andre / Vanya on 42nd Street
item.description:
In this captivating and philosophical film directed by Louis Malle, actor and playwright Wallace Shawn sits down with his friend the theater director André Gregory at a restaurant on New York’s Upper West Side, and the pair proceed through an alternately whimsical and despairing confessional about love, death, money, and all the superstition in between. Playing variations on their own New York–honed personas, Shawn and Gregory, who also cowrote the screenplay, dive in with introspective intellectual gusto, and Malle captures it all with a delicate, artful detachment. A fascinating freeze-frame of cosmopolitan culture, My Dinner with André remains a unique work in cinema history. (Janus Films) The collaborators of the acclaimed My Dinner with Andre (Louis Malle, Andre Gregory and Wallace Shawn) re-team for this update of Anton Checkhov’s Uncle Vanya, a timeless drama of faith, hope and the redemptive powers of love. Shawn and co-star Julianne Moore head the extraordinary ensemble cast in playwright David Mamet’s striking new adaptation of Checkhov’s drama of people trapped between the harsh reality of their lives and the longings of their hearts. Buffeted by a jaunty jazz score from Grammy nominee Joshua Redman, “this live-wire Vanya, freshly observed for the ‘90s is fiercely funny, touching and vital!” – Peter Travers, Rolling Stone (Sony Pictures Classics)
Cast: Director: Louis Malle | Writer: Screenplay by Wallace Shawn & Andre Gregory | Starring: Andre Gregory, Wallace Shawn | Year: 1981 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Louis Malle | Writer: From André Gregory's "Vanya" based on Anton Chekhov's "Uncle Vanya" adapted by David Mamet | Starring: Larry Pine, Brooke Smith, Wallace Shawn, André Gregory, Julianne Moore | Year: 1994 | Country: UK/USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 119 minuti

Orari: Thu August 13 - 7:30 pm / 9:50 pm

item.title: Cartoon Club
item.description:
Calling all animation aficionados! Join us for a celebration of the art and history of one of our favorite mediums with our monthly Cartoon Club! Enjoy a feature-length assemblage of classic shorts, including rare gems unearthed from the vaults and other animated antics lovingly assembled from our large archive of film treasures.

Orari: Thu July 30 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Misery / Dead Calm (Special Guests)
item.description:
While our allotment of advance tickets has sold out online, some additional tickets will be available to purchase at the door on a first come, first served basis on the night of the show. Box office & doors open approximately 1 hour before showtime. For shows that have sold out online, in-person ticket purchases are limited to 2 tickets per person. Paul Sheldon used to write for a living. Now, he’s writing to stay alive. When a famed novelist crashes his car during a blinding blizzard, he wakes to find himself at the mercy of his number one fan. Rob Reiner helms one of the best book-to-screen Stephen King adaptations, a delightfully cunning cat-and-mouse horror thriller fueled by a darkly comedic script from William Goldman and standout performances by James Caan and Academy Award winner Kathy Bates. “The actors are supported by the best kind of writerly craft and directorial technique, the kind that refuses to call attention to itself, never gets caught straining for scares or laughs. Popular moviemaking — elegantly economical, artlessly artful – doesn’t get much better than this.” – Richard Schickel, TIME “The movie has a real kick to it. As Paul and Annie attempt to outsmart each other, Misery gets nastier and nastier. It turns into a psychotic cat-and-mouse game, and there are some genuine shocks.” – Owen Gleiberman, Entertainment Weekly Just added: Dead Calm director Phillip Noyce and star Billy Zane will join us in person, schedules permitting, to pay tribute to their colleague & friend Sam Neill before their  film on Saturday, July 25th. When a veteran sailor and his wife rescue the half-delirious sole survivor of a crippled schooner on a dead-calm ocean, the skeptical husband rows to the disabled boat and finds a horrifying sight. Turning back to his own ship, he sees her racing away – with his wife on board – starting a desperate cat-and-mouse game on the high seas. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: Rob Reiner | Writer: Screenplay by William Goldman based on the novel by Stephen King | Starring: James Caan, Kathy Bates, Frances Sternhagen, Richard Farnsworth, Lauren Bacall | Year: 1990 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Phillip Noyce | Writer: Screenplay by Terry Hayes based on the novel by Charles Williams | Starring: Sam Neill, Nicole Kidman, Billy Zane | Year: 1989 | Country: Australia | Format: 35mm
Durata: 96 minuti

Orari: Sat July 25 - 6:00 pm / 8:15 pm
